description_long: This object is a long, straight-bladed sword with a double-edged design, featuring a pointed tip and a simple, unadorned crossguard. The blade appears to be forged from a dark, metallic material, giving it a sleek and formidable appearance. The handle is cylindrical and straight, ending in a rounded pommel, and seems to be made from the same material as the blade, with no visible wrapping or grip texture. The crossguard is a flat, straight bar perpendicular to the blade, providing basic hand protection. There are no visible engravings, inlays, or decorative elements on any part of the sword, emphasizing a utilitarian and functional design. The overall silhouette is reminiscent of a classic European longsword, suitable for both cutting and thrusting.
description: A long, straight, double-edged sword with a simple crossguard and a plain cylindrical handle, all in a dark metallic finish. The design is utilitarian with no visible ornamentation.
description_view_0: The sword is seen at an angle, highlighting the length of the blade and the straight crossguard.
description_view_1: A direct front view shows the full length of the blade, the symmetrical crossguard, and the centered handle and pommel.
description_view_2: The sword is viewed from the opposite angle, emphasizing the thinness of the blade and the alignment of the handle.
description_view_3: A rear view similar to the front, showing the straight blade, crossguard, and handle with minimal detail.
attribute_handle_design: straight cylindrical handle with rounded pommel, no visible wrapping or texture
